messages.content:
You are an expert Technical Product Manager.
Your job is to analyze the user's issue (Title and Body) along with the provided source code context, and generate a User Story, Acceptance Criteria, and Tasks.

OUTPUT FORMAT REQUIREMENTS:
You MUST output ONLY a valid JSON object. Do not wrap it in markdown. Do not include any explanations outside of the JSON.
The JSON must strictly follow this structure:
{
  "title": "Short title of the story",
  "story_type": "software_feature", 
  "jira_issue_type": "Story",
  "jira_labels": ["backend", "frontend", "bug", etc],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a [role], I want to [action] so that [benefit].",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given [precondition] When [action] Then [result]"
  ],
  "story_points": null, // Hoặc một số nguyên Fibonacci (1, 2, 3, 5, 8) đánh giá độ khó
  "tasks": {
    "be": ["Backend task 1", "Backend task 2"],
    "fe": ["Frontend task 1"],
    "qa": ["Test case 1"]
  },
  "definition_of_done": ["Code reviewed", "Tests passed"],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [],
  "assumptions": [],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": []
}

If the issue lacks so much context that it's impossible to deduce the required changes even with the provided code context, you may set "planning_status" to "NEEDS_CLARIFICATION" and provide your questions in "clarification_questions", leaving "tasks" empty. Otherwise, strive to generate concrete tasks.

ISSUE TITLE: AgentHost: No decorations for skills in chat input ISSUE BODY: --- RAG CONTEXT (SOURCE CODE) --- --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- # Agent Host The agent host communicates via the Agent Host Protocol. The specification for this lives in a directory `../agent-host-protocol` as a sibling of the VS Code directory. If this directory doesn't exist, you should use the "ask questions" tool to ask the user if they want to clone `git@github.com:microsoft/agent-host-protocol.git` to that directory. After doing so, you should also prompt the user to add `file:////plugins/copilot-plugin` as a plugin in their `chat.pluginLocations` settings. --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- ### `common/` - `chatService/` - IChatService interface, implementation, and related code. - `model/` - Chat data model, view model, and session storage. - `participants/` - Chat participant management (sometimes called "agents" in code). - `tools/` - Language model tools infrastructure and services. - `builtinTools/` - Implementations of some built-in tools. ===== FILE: D:\VSss-codedata\vscode\src\vs\workbench\contrib\imageCarousel\AGENTS.md ===== --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- # Agent host system-prompt customization This directory customizes the system prompt for Copilot CLI **agent host** (ahp+cli) sessions. Read this before changing how the system message is built or adding per-model / per-tool guidance. It mirrors the Copilot extension's `extensions/copilot/.../prompts/node/agent/` (agentPrompts), but the agent host runs in its own process and cannot use prompt-tsx, so contributors return plain data the SDK accepts directly. --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- ## Integration Points **IPromptsService:** - `listPromptFilesForStorage(type, storage)` - Get files for a type/storage combo - `findAgentSkills()` - Get skills with names parsed from frontmatter - `onDidChangeCustomAgents` - Refresh on agent changes - `onDidChangeSlashCommands` - Refresh on command changes **PromptsType enum:** `instructions | prompt | agent | skill` **PromptsStorage enum:** `local` (workspace) | `user` | `extension` **AI Feature Gating:** View gated by `ChatContextKeys.enabled` (respects `chat.disableAIFeatures` setting) **Registration:** Import `./aiCustomization/aiCustomization.contribution.js` in `chat.contribution.ts` --- *Update this file when making architectural changes to the AI Customization view.* ===== FILE: D:\VSss-codedata\vscode\.github\instructions\chat.instructions.md ===== --- description: Chat feature area coding guidelines --- --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- # Instructions 1. **Always read the `sessions` skill first.** This is your primary source of truth for the sessions architecture. - Invoke `skill: "sessions"`. 2. Focus your work on `src/vs/sessions/`. 3. Avoid making changes to core VS Code files (`src/vs/workbench/`, `src/vs/platform/`, etc.) unless absolutely necessary for the agents window functionality. ===== FILE: D:\VSss-codedata\vscode\.github\instructions\agentHostTesting.instructions.md ===== --- description: Architecture documentation for VS Code AI Customization view. Use when working in `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/aiCustomization` applyTo: 'src/vs/platform/agentHost/**' ---

response:
```json
{
  "title": "Agent Host: Add skill decorations/suggestions in chat input",
  "story_type": "software_feature",
  "jira_issue_type": "Story",
  "jira_labels": ["agent-host", "chat-ui", "skills", "ui-improvement"],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a user interacting with Agent Host, I want to see skill suggestions and decorations in the chat input field so that I can quickly discover available agent capabilities and use them during conversations.",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given an active Agent Host session, when typing in the chat input, then skill suggestions should appear as inline decorations above the cursor",
    "Given a user selects a skill suggestion from the decoration list, then that skill should be inserted into the chat message and focus should remain on the input field",
    "Given no matching skills exist for current context, when typing in chat input, then an empty state or 'No skills found' message should display",
    "Skill decorations should show the skill name and a brief description extracted from frontmatter metadata",
    "The skill decoration UI should be consistent with existing VS Code AI skill suggestions pattern"
  ],
  "story_points": 5,
  "tasks": {
    "be": [
      "Review IPromptsService.findAgentSkills() implementation to understand how skills are fetched and parsed",
      "Create a new service endpoint or extend existing one to return agent skills with metadata (name, description)",
      "Implement skill decoration rendering logic in the chat input component",
      "Add cursor position tracking for dynamic decoration placement"
    ],
    "fe": [
      "Update chat input component to support inline decorations/suggestions",
      "Create skill suggestion UI component that renders above cursor position",
      "Implement click handler for selecting a skill from suggestions",
      "Add animations/transitions for smooth suggestion appearance and dismissal"
    ],
    "qa": [
      "Test skill decoration rendering with various agent configurations",
      "Verify empty state handling when no skills match context",
      "Validate skill selection inserts correct text into chat message",
      "Ensure decorations don't interfere with normal typing flow"
    ]
  },
  "definition_of_done": [
    "Skill suggestions appear in chat input for all active Agent Host sessions",
    "All existing tests pass, new unit tests added for skill decoration functionality",
    "UI matches design specification for skill suggestion decorations",
    "Code reviewed and merged to main branch"
  ],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [
    "Should the skill suggestions be context-aware (filtered based on current conversation topic)?",
    "What is the expected maximum number of skills shown in the suggestion list?",
    "Should there be keyboard navigation support for skill suggestions (arrow keys, Enter to select)?",
    "Are we reusing existing VS Code AI skill decoration infrastructure or building a new pattern?"
  ],
  "assumptions": [
    "The Agent Host system already has skills defined with frontmatter metadata",
    "There is an existing chat input component that can be extended for decorations",
    "Skill descriptions are available from the agent configuration/skills data structure"
  ],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": [
    "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - Agent Host system documentation",
    "D:\\VSss-codedata\\vscode\\.github\\instructions\\chat.instructions.md - Chat feature guidelines",
    "IPromptsService interface showing findAgentSkills() method"
  ]
}
```
